subject: Computer Networking
title: TLS for Email
description: A fast breakdown of TLS for Email for builders.

hook.text: Emails sent without encryption are readable by any server in the path. TLS fixes that.

buildup.text: STARTTLS upgrades an SMTP connection to encrypted. The mail flies in a TLS tunnel between servers.

discovery.text: MTA-STS forces TLS. Without it, a server can downgrade to plaintext and you'd never know.

twist.text: TLS encrypts the link, not the message. Each server decrypts, reads, then re-encrypts for the next hop.

climax.text: Google's transparency report shows 90%+ of Gmail traffic now uses TLS in transit. Huge progress.

punchline.text: TLS protects email in transit. Not perfect, but essential.

quiz.question: What does STARTTLS do for an SMTP connection?
options: Upgrades it from plaintext to encrypted TLS
Compresses the email to reduce size
Adds a digital signature to the message
